name: ai-client-governance
description: Reusable agent-neutral governance plugin layer for AI coding clients, embedded as a complete Git repository.

rulesLayout.commonEntrySemantics: canonical agent-neutral governance contract exposed through an adapter filename

pathLayout.placeholderPolicy: Use placeholders in generated docs and command examples; use real relative Markdown links for clickable references.

mandatoryInputFilter.preWorktreeApplyPolicy: Input-filter task records may be applied before worktree evidence; prewrite/final gates must enforce worktree evidence before repository writes or closeout.

mandatoryCommandCompression.capabilityPolicy: task-run plan exposes per-command capability, risk_level, side_effect, cache_eligible, parallel_eligible, approval_required, approval_reason, policy_decision, policy_severity, and policy_findings as planner facts; task-run run fails before execution on block or unapproved approval_required decisions by default.
mandatoryCommandCompression.cachePolicy: Cache is opt-in and limited to readonly/validation nodes; stateful, sequential, Git write, lock, task-record apply, git status, git diff, and git worktree list commands are no-cache.
mandatoryCommandCompression.telemetryPolicy: task-run run, gate-pool, shell-adapter, telemetry record, and the command adapter write execution telemetry spans/events to aicg.db by default; --jsonl-artifact-dir is only an explicit isolated artifact/export path and --no-telemetry is only for isolated tests. Commands use span_kind=command and subject_type=command; future model HTTP, sub-agent, token usage, and external API statistics must extend the same execution_spans/execution_events schema. Each governed span should carry client_type and model_id attributes when available; unknown values stay explicit for later audit. Reports expose command-error failure categories, classified/unclassified counts, command_file_required counts, and inline-command warnings. Windows PowerShell mandatory command coverage uses shell-adapter proxy-powershell; risky inline PowerShell is rewritten to a temporary UTF-8 command file by default, while --no-auto-command-file plus --fail-on-inline-risk blocks risky inline execution before subprocess launch. task-run run, gate-pool, shell-adapter run, or the command adapter provide wrapper telemetry for other governed command paths. Raw host shell calls must be justified when not covered. Telemetry-wrapped commands are compensation evidence but do not clear raw_shell_gap; no-profile command proxy telemetry or local env activation closes raw shell coverage for governed commands without changing user profiles, PATH, terminal settings, or global shell behavior. PowerShell profile shims are explicit opt-in only. Use task-run diagnose --require-raw-shell-coverage or shell-adapter diagnose --require-raw-shell-coverage for fail-closed checks.

structuredRecords.markdownPolicy: Markdown task tracking is a human-readable export or historical audit artifact, not a gate input for new tasks.
structuredRecords.readOnlyPolicy: task-record status never creates the SQLite database; missing DB returns an empty summary.

designPrinciples.summary: Govern host AI coding client capabilities without replacing the client, model, or agent runtime.
Keep common rules, scripts, skills, README, and manifest together as an embedded Git repository.
Treat AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, Copilot, Cursor, Cline, Windsurf, Continue, Roo, Trae, and Aider files as thin adapters to the same facts.
Turn repeatable, checkable, or state-changing constraints into CLI commands, runtime components, gates, state files, or skills before expanding prose rules.
Analyze new local command candidates for dedupe, batching, gate-pool aggregation, telemetry wrapping, or cache reuse before spending additional model turns.
Choose reuse, spawn, merge, or close for sub-agents from task tree, write-scope locks, context capsules, and token budget evidence instead of a fixed small agent cap.
Require worktrees, task tracking, locks, tool telemetry, validation evidence, and final state reporting for modification tasks.
Keep project business rules, resumes, learning paths, source snapshots, delivery details, and private preferences in the host project layer.
Preserve existing native rule entries, skills, IDE configuration, and team conventions unless the user explicitly approves changes.
Detect missing, dirty, ahead, behind, or diverged states without automatically pulling, pushing, merging, or deleting.
Use task tracking, state files, and logs for recovery, but re-check live worktree, Git, session, lock, and queue state before final decisions.
Write common rules in agent-neutral terms and keep tool-specific behavior inside local adapters or skills.

runtimeArchitecture.name: ai-client-governance-plugin
runtimeArchitecture.concept: Spring-style governance plugin layer for AI coding clients. It assumes the host client provides conversation, tool execution, file writes, shell commands, approvals, and session recovery; AI Client Governance governs how those capabilities are used.
runtimeArchitecture.hostClientAssumption: Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Windsurf, Continue, and similar clients already provide the agent runtime surface. AI Client Governance supplies policy, gates, evidence, and state audits on top of that surface.
notAReplacementFor: LangGraph workflow engine unless using a self-built runner

requiredEvidence: review pass/fail result by task id
agentContextReusePolicy.defaultRule: reuse active same-task same-scope agents when fresh and uncontaminated; spawn for independent isolated leaves; merge when scopes overlap; close completed agents after writing a context capsule; never pass full history when a capsule and line-bounded reads are sufficient

runtimeArchitecture.extensionPolicy: Add deterministic behavior as registered governance components before expanding prose rules.

documentImpactPolicy.trigger: rules, scripts, skills, manifest, README, adapters, or Markdown changes that may affect user-facing docs or references
documentImpactPolicy.trackingEvidence: record affected docs and references, updated files, or explicit no-impact rationale in the structured task record

executionGuarantees.componentVisibility: python .ai-client/ai-client-governance/scripts/ai_client_governance.py runtime components must show matching nodes for the task context
executionGuarantees.gateExecution: python .ai-client/ai-client-governance/scripts/ai_client_governance.py gate-pool --dry-run must show gate steps without mutating state; actual runs require a task id or historical tracking source
executionGuarantees.dedupePolicy: gate-pool de-duplicates by gate_step and aggregates changed paths before running the same gate
executionGuarantees.performancePolicy: avoid unconditional checks; every expensive node needs event, task type, path, final-only, or changed-path triggers
executionGuarantees.worktreeCreationPolicy: plan-output/write-intent/resume must expose worktree-creation-policy, requiring worktree-task create by default plus sparse/source snapshot handling evidence; raw git worktree add is break-glass only
executionGuarantees.worktreeLiveStatePolicy: worktree-task reconcile treats Git worktree list as the source of truth and blocks active coord sessions whose worktree disappeared
executionGuarantees.inputFilterPreflightPolicy: user-message must pass lifecycle input-filter and task-record gate --event preflight before later processing; missing input-filter.preflight and capability-gateway.facts fail closed for mutating work
executionGuarantees.clientIdentityPolicy: lifecycle input-filter must record events.event_type=client-identity.analysis with client_type and model_id so task-record and telemetry reports can identify which AI clients or models skipped the standard flow; unknown identity is explicit and warning-worthy, not silently omitted.
executionGuarantees.commandCompressionPolicy: medium, large, or mutating tasks must record command-compression.analysis before write-intent or final gates; runtime components include a mutating-task fast path so small repository writes cannot skip the node; task-run plan provides the local dedupe, grouping, and per-command capability/risk/side-effect event payload
executionGuarantees.securityPolicy: policy assess and the security-policy gate provide deterministic local checks for prompt injection, sensitive information, command injection, supply-chain, Git write, destructive command, network, and path-boundary risk; host-native raw shell interception remains outside the plugin, so governed command paths and diagnostics fail closed instead of claiming runtime-level interception. Raw shell governance must use non-invasive local command proxy or env activation by default, not user profile or global shell mutation.
executionGuarantees.taskRunExecutionPolicy: task-run run executes one compressed local DAG, parallelizes only readonly/validation groups, writes telemetry events to aicg.db by default, stores trace JSON when requested, and blocks block or unapproved approval_required policy decisions before subprocess execution.
executionGuarantees.taskRunCachePolicy: task-run cache keys include runner version, command, cwd, node kind, task types, changed paths, declared input hashes, and Git HEAD; cache hits still write telemetry events
executionGuarantees.taskRunDiagnosticsPolicy: task-run diagnose reports terminal duplicate commands, classified/unclassified command failure categories, command-file-required failure counts, telemetry failures, cache hit/miss counts, coord locks/sessions, missing worktrees, task-record/task-queue alignment, current task presence, local shell-adapter env activation, optional explicit profile shim markers, no-profile command proxy evidence, shell-adapter telemetry, telemetry-wrapped command evidence, and raw shell coverage gaps; --task-id/--trace-id/--since/--until narrow reports to current scope; --require-raw-shell-coverage exits non-zero when neither local env activation nor command proxy evidence is present
executionGuarantees.telemetryEffectivenessPolicy: telemetry report exposes OpenTelemetry/W3C Trace Context style report-layer mapping, compact latest spans, and command-error aggregation; telemetry effectiveness compares before/after spans without migrating the SQLite schema, and effectiveness snapshot/trend writes reusable metrics to governance_state instead of JSON live files.
executionGuarantees.taskLifecycleViewPolicy: task-queue lifecycle is a unified view over task queue and structured task record statuses; completed and done normalize to lifecycle done while missing/status/trace drift remains visible, and task-queue transition is the explicit write path for updating both stores together.
executionGuarantees.structuredRecordPolicy: new tasks should call contract describe before execution and write typed SQLite task records before preflight/final gates; input-filter records can be applied before worktree creation, while final gates require worktree evidence for mutating tasks; task-id mode does not require Markdown task tracking
executionGuarantees.entrypointResolutionPolicy: runtime command generators and diagnostics resolve only embedded .ai-client/ai-client-governance or governance-repo-local scripts/ai_client_governance.py; host-root scripts/ai_client_governance.py is not a fallback
executionGuarantees.hostSubmoduleCloseoutPolicy: after merging an embedded ai-client-governance worktree, host closeout must verify and commit the .ai-client/ai-client-governance gitlink, aicg.db worktree state, and related task tracking
executionGuarantees.completionTestPolicy: completion-test plans required validation from changed paths, task types, and acceptance criteria
executionGuarantees.agentReviewResultPolicy: implemented for task-gate, task-record gate --event final, runtime component registration, and selftests; pending for worktree closeout, host-closeout, and agent-groups/agent-comm status: multi-agent execution chains require reviewer Agent pass/fail results for each executor Agent task id or leaf id before merge, closeout, or task-queue transition to done; result facts use agent-neutral client_type fields and must include reviewer_agent, reviewer_client_type, executor_agent, executor_client_type, reviewed task id or leaf id, pass/fail, lifecycle fact check, commit status check, unhandled items, low-quality items, evidence, remediation guidance, retest plan, and retest result; lifecycle fact checks must cover task queue lifecycle, task record status, requirements/triggers/outputs/worktrees/validations/events, final gate, worktree live state, branch/head, dirty status, commit/merge/push status, validation results, telemetry/raw shell gap, and active/pending state; fail results, stale lifecycle records, unresolved P0/P1 issues, missing remediation, missing client_type, non-independent reviewer, or missing retest evidence block merge
executionGuarantees.outputBoundaryPolicy: Git push state is audited at plan-output, status-output, and final-output boundaries; no automatic push
executionGuarantees.lifecycleSopPolicy: non-readonly mutating/rules/docs/git/correction/multi-agent/long-running tasks must follow sync-check, lifecycle input-filter, task-queue, task-record apply/gate, worktree-task create, preflight, task-run or gate-pool execution, completion-test, final gate, optional worktree closeout, then task-queue transition --to done; each phase must produce structured facts
executionGuarantees.taskRecordQueueCompletionPolicy: task-queue transition --to done is allowed only after the task record exists, final gate passes, requirements are closed, worktree live state is recorded, validation and telemetry facts exist, multi-agent reviewer result requirements pass task-record final gate or are recorded as blocked/follow-up, and no unexplained dirty worktree, missing_in_task_record, command-error, policy-blocked command, or raw shell gap remains
executionGuarantees.multiWorktreeFactsPolicy: one active root task may own multiple worktrees, but each worktree must record root_task_id, task_node_id or leaf id, repo, slug, branch, base commit, owner agent, write scope, forbidden paths, validation command, return capsule, review pass/fail status, commit/merge/push status, integration owner, conflict matrix, and next action
executionGuarantees.commandErrorTaxonomyPolicy: unexpected command failures must be recorded as command-error.analysis or equivalent facts with failed_command, exit_code, phase, parser_or_shell, failure_category, root_cause, corrected_command, retry_count, dedupe_key, preventive_rule, telemetry evidence, state impact, and framework-debt decision; runtime classification covers python -c inline quoting, inline JSON, complex inline PowerShell, governance argparse usage errors, Git failures, cwd misuse, and PowerShell command-not-found patterns
executionGuarantees.gitWriteGovernancePolicy: shell-adapter proxy-powershell proves shell telemetry only; git add, commit, merge, push, rm, and mv must also pass policy assess, task-run run, or higher-level worktree-task/host-closeout paths, while direct raw git writes require break-glass evidence
executionGuarantees.artifactCleanupPolicy: selftest, doc-index, completion-test, gate-pool, temporary probes, pycache, cache, lifecycle, and tmp outputs must have owner command, artifact manifest, allowed path, and cleanup/reconcile policy; undeclared artifacts in a task worktree are dirty blockers
executionGuarantees.enforcementBeforeDocumentationPolicy: requirements that need enforcement must first be implemented or recorded as framework-debt/follow-up implementation work with design_only status; documentation cannot claim runtime enforcement until code, adapter, policy, gate, or schema validation exists
executionGuarantees.designHandoffReviewPolicy: design-only tasks must persist a design package in the task record with goals, non-goals, architecture, data model, policy/gate plan, migration/cleanup plan, validation plan, risks, implementation tasks, reviewer acceptance criteria, agent review pass/fail criteria, and handoff capsule; execution and review tasks must link back to the root task
executionGuarantees.staleHumanReportCleanupPolicy: old Markdown tracking, pending summaries, correction indexes, chat logs, and exported reports are audit or migration inputs only; current task/problem/worktree state must come from SQLite and Git live state, and misleading current-state human reports require cleanup instead of fallback readers
executionGuarantees.docImpactBubblingPolicy: documentation changes must bubble impact from changed paths to local README, .references, parent README or AGENTS, manifest, command docs, and inbound links before widening to global doc-index checks; doc-impact or no-impact facts must be recorded

agentEntryAdapters.nativeCompatNote: CodeBuddy auto-loads root AGENTS.md when CODEBUDDY.md is absent; this adapter is the dedicated .codebuddy/rules entry. RULE.mdc uses YAML frontmatter (description, alwaysApply, enabled).

hostTracked.commonRepo: .ai-client/ai-client-governance as a Git gitlink/submodule entry only

gitignorePolicy.managedBlockBegin: # BEGIN AI Client Governance generated runtime
gitignorePolicy.managedBlockEnd: # END AI Client Governance generated runtime

audit.closeoutPolicy: closeout-all may stage the embedded governance gitlink and explicit task-tracking paths only; it must not git add -f live DB, legacy JSON state, logs, caches, or task worktrees.
strictFailures: tracked normal file inside the common governance repo

repoContents.commonRulesSemantics: agent-neutral governance contract exposed through the AGENTS.md adapter

existingFilesPolicy.agentAdapters: not-generated-by-default; create-missing-with-InstallAgentAdapters; preserve native files; backup-and-upgrade legacy ai-client generated adapters; rewrite all with ForceAgentAdapters

oldLayoutPolicy: The previous .codex governance layout is not supported. Do not generate adapters, state, records, tools, skills, or fallbacks there; if active docs or scripts reference it, replace them with .ai-client paths. Historical audit records may mention old paths only as past facts.

adapterPolicy.existingAdapterPolicy: not-generated-by-default; preserve native existing adapters when installing; backup-and-upgrade stale or legacy ai-client generated adapters; rewrite all only with ForceAgentAdapters
